races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Claiming Hurdle

runners.name: Desert Cave
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over fences and in point-to-points lately; has to return to best hurdles form of 2024; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Well beaten over fences and in points lately; has to return to best hurdles form of 2024

runners.name: Oh So Charming
runners.spotlight: Sole win over hurdles came in a 2m4f Clonmel handicap last June off 111; some fair efforts in defeat since; fourth in a Clonmel claimer in February; has a squeak if back to best.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Clonmel claimer in February; has a squeak on these terms if back to best

runners.name: You Done Well
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; won a 2m3f Limerick handicap off 100 last summer; not as good since and has a bit to find on these terms; visor added.
runners.comment: Won a 2m3f Limerick handicap off 100 last summer; not as good since; visor added

runners.name: Tell Us This
runners.spotlight: Has been hit and miss in defeat over fences in last six months; won over hurdles at Downpatrick last summer off 100 but not as good off a higher mark; needs best.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles last summer off 100 but not as good off a higher mark; needs best

runners.name: War Correspondent
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort showed his best form last autumn over fences, including third in a Punchestown Grade 3 novice; rated 10lb less over hurdles but can be involved if ready enough on return.
runners.comment: Best form last autumn over fences; rated less over hurdles but can be involved on return

runners.name: Redwood Queen
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since joining this yard last year after unseating in a Wexford claimer but some decent efforts in defeat; unseated on chasing debut when last seen; a sound chance if back to her best.
runners.comment: Unseated on chasing debut when last seen;  a sound chance if back to her best

runners.name: Hasten Slowly
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins over hurdles have come over C&D including her last score in August; well below her best on AW last three runs but respected back over flights here; best form is on good ground, though.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; not at best on AW but respected back in this sphere; best on good ground

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Maiden Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Crooked Path
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden has run with credit in defeat in most of his 10 hurdle starts; third at Limerick last time and a repeat of that will see him go close; ground-versatile.
runners.comment: Third at Limerick last time and a repeat of that will see him go close; ground-versatile

runners.name: Le Tatoue Madrik
runners.spotlight: Best effort in three hurdle runs when third at Fairyhouse in February; fell halfway at Gowran since; seems to be the yard's first-string; cheekpieces added; looks a player.
runners.comment: Best hurdle run when third at Fairyhouse in February; cheekpieces added; looks a player

runners.name: Baby Fish
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; third runner for yard; beaten a long way in three maidens and handicaps will suit better in time
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in three maidens and handicaps will suit better in time; reserve

runners.name: Inchidaly Rua
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart 2m5f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Inchidaly Rock; doesn't make that much appeal on debut.
runners.comment: Doesn't make that much appeal on debut

runners.name: Goodmancon
runners.spotlight: Nine bumper starts; best effort came when third at this track in 2024; not far off that form at Clonmel when last seen; probably find a few too strong on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Best of nine bumper runs came here in 2024; probably find a few too strong on hurdle debut

runners.name: Nameyourgame
runners.spotlight: Third in a point on debut but was pulled-up next time and then beaten 48l in a Clonmel maiden hurdle; has to improve hugely on that effort.
runners.comment: Beaten 48l in a Clonmel maiden hurdle; has to improve hugely on that effort

runners.name: Whet The Appetite
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in a Cork maiden on recent debut and can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in a Cork maiden on recent debut and can only be watched for now

runners.name: Crystal Yeats
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in two points including by the classy Ballyfad; beaten a long way on hurdles debut recently so best watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on hurdles debut recently so best watched for now

runners.name: Davitts Quay
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in two maiden hurdles and best watched with a view to handicaps.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in two maiden hurdles and best watched with a view to handicaps

runners.name: Cornaroya Rocco
runners.spotlight: Not much to show for his three maiden hurdle runs and handicaps will suit better in time.
runners.comment: Not much to show for his three maiden hurdle runs and handicaps will suit better in time

runners.name: Mor Go Leor
runners.spotlight: Point winner last year but has been well beaten in two maiden hurdles after an absence; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Point winner has been well beaten in two maiden hurdles after an absence;  best watched

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Maiden Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: The Monkey Pole
runners.spotlight: Was runner-up in maiden hurdles at Wexford (2m1f) and here (2m5f) last August; beaten 5l on latter occasion by a subsequent Grade 3 winner; was poor in a bumper when last seen but is a big player if back to best form on return.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two maiden hurdles last August incl' here; big player if back to that form

runners.name: Quiet In Bresil
runners.spotlight: First reserve; the best of two hurdle runs was on debut at Cork; beaten 78l there since and has to resume progress.
runners.comment: The best of two hurdle runs was on debut at Cork; has to resume progress;  reserve

runners.name: Le Questionnaire
runners.spotlight: Placed in both point starts in UK but was beaten a long way on hurdles debut at Fairyhouse recently; respect for connections the  reason he is worth a second look.
runners.comment: Placed in both point starts in UK but beaten a long way on hurdles debut

runners.name: Deep Warrior
runners.spotlight: Some promise on hurdles debut at Cork but tailed-off at Fairyhouse since so is best watched.
runners.comment: Some promise on hurdles debut but tailed-off since so best watched

runners.name: Thilos
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; regressive on the Flat for Joseph O'Brien; beaten 24l on handicap hurdle debut at Fairyhouse so needs plenty more to take a maiden.
runners.comment: Beaten 24l on h'cap debut at Fairyhouse so needs plenty more; reserve

runners.name: Time Is A Thief
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up (beaten a neck by the classy Pure Steel) ran well before weakening then falling on hurdle debut at Cork; is better than and his local yard is in winning form so should go well; hooded.
runners.comment: Point runner-up ran well before weakening on hurdle debut; the ability to go well; hooded

runners.name: China Grill
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to point winner Amyr; dam point winner, out of unraced half-sister to 3m hurdle winners What's In Front, Sandysaran and Executive Star; by a useful sire; unsold for Eur9,500 as a yearling; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: By a useful sire; unsold for Eur9,500 as a yearling; best watched on debut

runners.name: King Of The Greys
runners.spotlight: Ran well on sole point start but was tailed-off on hurdles debut lately so has to improve hugely on that run.
runners.comment: Ran well sole point start but tailed-off on hurdles debut lately

runners.name: Lord Lackendarra
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in some of his 10 bumpers including here; unseated at the first on hurdles debut last October; could run well back at this venue.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in some of his bumpers incl' here; unseated at 1st on hurdles debut

runners.name: Knowing Me
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in two bumpers and three maiden hurdles so isn't easy to make a case for in another maiden.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in two bumpers and three maiden hurdles so isn't easy to make a case for

runners.name: Lanzhou
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in two points but has been well beaten in two maiden hurdles and handicaps are likely to suit better; 10th here yesterday.
runners.comment: Some ability in two points but well beaten in two maiden hurdles

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Prince Of Air
runners.spotlight: One hurdle and two chase wins at this venue; was demoted from first in a Killarney chase last autumn; has a chance if back to form after two poor chase efforts for this new yard.
runners.comment: One hurdle and two chase wins here; chance if back to form after two poor chase efforts

runners.name: Bossing It
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last summer over 2m and was third in handicaps here (2m5f) and Galway (2m); likely found the ground too testing in three runs since; a chance back on a drier surface.
runners.comment: Won a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last summer and 3rd in h'caps here and Galway; a chance

runners.name: Mollys Dolly
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; not a bad handicap debut at Roscommon last July; pulled-up on return at Cork.
runners.comment: Not a bad handicap debut at Roscommon last July; pulled-up on return at Cork; reserve

runners.name: Maxicourt
runners.spotlight: Best run was when second to the smart Cousin Kate in a C&D maiden last October; nowhere near that form since and needs his best on return.
runners.comment: Best run when 2nd to the smart Cousin Kate in a C&D maiden; not near that form since

runners.name: Liss Eile
runners.spotlight: Some decent bumper runs in defeat; her best hurdle run came at this track last year; well below that lately; has to repeat best form.
runners.comment: Some decent bumper runs; best hurdle run came at this track last year; has to repeat that

runners.name: Kimy
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 23 hurdle starts came here in 2024; plenty of decent runs in defeat since including in Listed company; beaten 11l at Cork last time off 2lb higher; needs more.
runners.comment: Sole win in 23 hurdle starts came here in 2024; beaten 11l at Cork last time; needs more

runners.name: Synchronize
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden showed some ability in maiden hurdles but was poor in two handicaps including here last year; has to return to that earlier form.
runners.comment: Some ability in maidens but was poor in two handicaps incl' here last year

runners.name: Sheer Raz
runners.spotlight: Has posted decent runs in defeat the last twice over further, notably last time in a higher grade at Fairyhouse (2m4f); can be closely involved if as effective over this trip.
runners.comment: Decent runs last twice over further and can be involved if as effective over this trip

runners.name: Just For One
runners.spotlight: Second in a Limerick maiden in December but was pulled-up in two handicaps since so has major questions to answer now.
runners.comment: Second in a Limerick maiden but pulled-up in two handicaps since so questions

runners.name: Liberated Light
runners.spotlight: Just 1-18 over hurdles; has been poor on heavy ground lately but this drier surface can help him put in a better showing; blinkers added.
runners.comment: Just 1-18 over hurdles; poor on heavy ground lately but drier surface can help

runners.name: Kilbarry Ce Ce
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well in defeat at this track in a maiden and handicap hurdle; poor when last seen (in cheekpieces) but has a chance if ready enough on return; ground-versatile.
runners.comment: Has run well in defeat at this track in a maiden and handicap; a chance if ready enough
runners.owner: Mrs Con O'Keeffe & Tadhg O'Sullivan & Anthony O'Keeffe

runners.name: Regno
runners.spotlight: First reserve; well beaten in three maiden hurdles but can do better in this company; check the market if getting in.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles but can do better in this company; reserve

runners.name: Mount Hotham
runners.spotlight: Was well beaten in four maiden hurdles and has to do much better after a break even dropping to this level.
runners.comment: Well beaten in four maiden hurdles and has to do much better after a break

runners.name: Go Gill Go
runners.spotlight: Just 1-34 over hurdles and her regression continues so not much appeal until showing more spark.
runners.comment: Just 1-34 over hurdles and her regression continues so not much appeal

runners.name: Cholo
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far over C&D on handicap debut last August; needs to improve on that form on his first run since.
runners.comment: Not beaten far over C&D on h'cap debut last August; needs a repeat on first run since

runners.name: Stumblin In
runners.spotlight: Her sole win came in a 2m5f handicap at this meeting last year; however, she lost her form badly after and needs to get back to her best on return.
runners.comment: Sole win came in a 2m5f handicap at this meeting last year; lost form after

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jetaway Nana
runners.spotlight: Stays further than this; not beaten far in fourth at Fairyhouse in November, absent since but not without a chance; seems to be ground-versatile, although her Wexford win was gained on good going.
runners.comment: Stays further than this, not beaten far in fourth at Fairyhouse in November, absent since

runners.name: Fair Damsel
runners.spotlight: Won Killarney maiden hurdle in October 2024 (2m1f, yielding); ran only twice last year; reappeared in a claimer at Clonmel in February; probably best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Ran only twice last year, reappeared in a claimer, probably best watched on stable debut

runners.name: Navy Waves
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in four starts since a 20-1 Galway win last autumn; remote sixth in a 3m handicap at Cork last month on first run after a break.
runners.comment: Unplaced in four starts since a 20-1 Galway win last autumn, others much preferred

runners.name: Bearami Creek
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m3f at Navan last September and decent efforts in defeat at Listowel and Punchestown before a break; recent Clonmel run looked too bad to be true even though the 2m trip was inadequate.
runners.comment: Recent Clonmel run looked too bad to be true even though the 2m trip was inadequate

runners.name: Dancing After Dark
runners.spotlight: Won over 3m at Limerick in November 2024; ran only once last year; shaped with promise when fourth at Punchestown after a long break; failed to build on that at Cork last month; cheekpieces now added to tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Fair fourth at Punchestown after a long break, failed to build on that at Cork last month

runners.name: Turkey And Ham
runners.spotlight: Absent since winning at Kilbeggan a year ago; might prefer better ground and is entitled to need the run; however, she is only 4lb higher than for her win and is worth a market check with Oran McGill claiming a valuable 5lb.
runners.comment: Absent since winning at Kilbeggan a year ago, a valuable 5lb claimed, worth a market check

runners.name: Might Be The One
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of her last four races, every chance if a step up in trip does the trick after a recent second placing in a 16-runner 2m contest at Clonmel; more mature now than when fading in the closing stages over C&D last November.
runners.comment: Placed in three of her last four races, every chance if a step up in trip does the trick

runners.name: Yoursimplythebest
runners.spotlight: Showed next to nothing in maidens; found the right level in handicaps; placed on her first three starts; second over 3m at Punchestown seemed very encouraging, but she failed to do herself justice when joint-favourite at Fairyhouse in January.
runners.comment: Failed to do herself justice at Fairyhouse in January, decent chance on previous form

runners.name: Cocoplum
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden who has changed stable since her last run in August 2024; makes no appeal after such a long absence.
runners.comment: 18-race maiden who has changed stable since her last run in August 2024, makes no appeal

runners.name: Whitewinewednesday
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; her staying-on third at Navan last September (2m3f, yielding) was encouraging; failed to build on that on her last two starts before a break; needs to find extra now.
runners.comment: Point winner, managed a couple of good runs last autumn, needs to find extra after a break

runners.name: La Rocca Lass
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; Placed only once in seven bumper starts; tailed off at huge odds in four maiden hurdles; tailed off on handicap debut at Cllonmel last Tuesday.
runners.comment: Second reserve, struggled in four maiden hurdles; tailed off on handicap debut at Clonmel

runners.name: Lakewood Rose
runners.spotlight: 1-8 in points, fair bumper form, some merit in her sixth placing in maiden hurdles at Naas and Cork; could go well at a decent price in her first handicap.
runners.comment: Fair bumper form, some merit in her sixth placing in maiden hurdles at Naas and Cork

runners.name: Barrowline
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in point-to-points; three-figure odds for four maiden hurdle outings and ran accordingly; always at the back and pulled up before two out at Naas on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Always at the back and pulled up before two out at Naas on handicap debut

runners.name: Ballinaboola Gold
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Naas and Clonmel on her first two handicap attempts, 25-1 both times; has time on her side but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Down the field at Naas and Clonmel on her first two handicap attempts, 25-1 both times

runners.name: Lady Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner at Down Royal two years ago; generally modest form since then; blinkered now
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner two years ago, generally modest form since then, blinkered now

runners.name: Ballinagaine Girl
runners.spotlight: Nothing in the form of her three maiden hurdle runs hints at a featuring role here on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Nothing in the form of her three maiden hurdle runs hints at a featuring role here

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Beginners Chase

runners.name: Gunnery Sergeant
runners.spotlight: Second on his two most recent maiden hurdle starts but the form does not amount to much; beaten 22l by a stablemate of Drop A Threat at Downpatrick last time.
runners.comment: Second on his two most recent maiden hurdle starts but the form does not amount to much

runners.name: Drop A Threat
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin point winner for Donnchadh Doyle two seasons ago; failed to deliver over hurdles; switch to chasing may help, and the bar is set low for this introduction.
runners.comment: Point winner for a top team, failed to deliver over hurdles, switch to chasing may help

runners.name: Jumping Susie
runners.spotlight: 3-16 over hurdles for John McConnell, the wins coming at 2m3f-2m5f; has failed to match that over fences; has changed to a leading local stable; talented 5lb claimer rides.
runners.comment: Useful form over hurdles, has failed to match that over fences, has changed stable

runners.name: Faceman
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins last year, a maiden at Kilbeggan (good) and a three-runner novice event at this venue (good); 110-rated gelding out of the great Solerina; interesting on chase debut given his status as a course winner.
runners.comment: Course winner of a three-runner hurdle race last August, interesting on chase debut

runners.name: Magic Day
runners.spotlight: Ran with credit in maidens before getting off the mark in 2m3f Kilbeggan handicap hurdle last April (yielding); disappointing at Wexford on chase debut; very poor run over hurdles at Punchestown last time.
runners.comment: Handicap hurdle winner, flopped at Wexford on chase debut, poor run over hurdles last time

runners.name: Warriors Wood
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Kilbeggan and Ballinrobe on good ground early in the season; absent since last August; soft going may not be ideal for his chasing debut.
runners.comment: Won twice over hurdles on good ground early in the season, soft going may not be ideal

runners.name: River Tara
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; placed in three maiden hurdles for Willie Mullins; questions to answer having been pulled up on both starts for this stable.
runners.comment: Placed twice over hurdles for Willie Mullins, pulled up on both starts for this stable

runners.name: Black Soul
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 22 starts came in a 2m1f maiden hurdle at Down Royal last May (good) for John and Thomas Kiely; has struggled lately over hurdles and on AW at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Point and maiden hurdle winner, has struggled lately over hurdles and on AW at Dundalk

runners.name: Ira Hayes
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner of a Wexford bumper (2m, soft) and a Carlisle maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to soft) in 2024; has failed to prosper since then; last of 11 finishers in a handicap chase at Leopardstown seven weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won a Wexford bumper and Carlisle maiden hurdle in late 2024, has failed to prosper since

runners.name: Charity Barnum
runners.spotlight: Best hurdles run when second at this venue last August, no impact in two races over fences.
runners.comment: Best hurdles run when second at this venue last August, no impact in two races over fences

runners.name: Written In My Soul
runners.spotlight: 85-rated hurdler, last of five finishers at Down Royal on chase debut, can be left out of calculations
runners.comment: 85-rated hurdler, last of five finishers on chase debut, can be left out of calculations

runners.name: The Goose's Acre
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles, rated 92; last of nine on chase debut at Clonmel last week; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles, rated 92; last of nine on chase debut at Clonmel last week

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Handicap Chase

runners.name: Toor Moon
runners.spotlight: Consistent form last autumn, finishing placed five times in a row, two over hurdles; runner-up in his first three handicap chase starts, including over C&D; lost form for a while before a reassuring effort over hurdles at Downpatrick; a leading contender.
runners.comment: Three seconds in handicap chases late last year, third over hurdles on latest, good chance

runners.name: Ballybrack Wood
runners.spotlight: Scored at this venue on his chase debut in October 2024; soundly beaten by a longer-priced stablemate at Naas before out of the money at Leopardstown; missed all of 2025; could feature for top local and stable if fit and ready after long layoff.
runners.comment: Course winner in October 2024, missed all of 2025, could feature if fit and ready

runners.name: Another Ocana
runners.spotlight: First reserve; best form at Limerick; needs to step up on recent form at that venue.
runners.comment: First reserve, best form at Limerick, needs to step up on recent form at that venue

runners.name: Castle Croiuil
runners.spotlight: Point winner two seasons ago; stand-out effort on the track when 15l second in a  2m4f maiden hurdle at Limerick last November, chase form provides little encouragement.
runners.comment: Second over hurdles at Limerick last November, chase form provides little encouragement

runners.name: Cullenwaine
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-18 over fences; lost his form for Philip Rothwell last summer but fair effort on yard debut at this venue on New Year's Day; not as good at Thurles last time.
runners.comment: Hard to win with, fair run on stable debut at this venue, not as good at Thurles last time

runners.name: Oogum Boogum
runners.spotlight: 0-18 over hurdles; recent third on his chasing debut in a handicap at Wexford offers some hope of a breakthrough success.
runners.comment: 0-18 over hurdles, recent third in a handicap chase at Wexford offers some hope

runners.name: Ossifer Hops
runners.spotlight: Won a Downpatrick maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2023; modest record overall; 1-17 over hurdles, 0-9 in chases, hard to make a case for him on recent hurdles form.
runners.comment: 1-17 over hurdles, 0-9 in chases, hard to make a case for him on recent hurdles form

runners.name: Likable Chancer
runners.spotlight: 3-34 over fences, with two of those successes coming at this track, including when 33-1 chance in this race in 2023; creditable third in a 2m7f handicap chase here on New Year's Day; flopped when well fancied for a handicap hurdle at Limerick last time.
runners.comment: Flopped when well fancied for a handicap hurdle last time, has won twice at this venue

runners.name: Lake Chad
runners.spotlight: Has run many of his best races at this track, three wins and three seconds from 14 visits; poor run at Wexford last month; the balance of this season's form has been respectable, and his liking for this circuit is a significant positive
runners.comment: Poor run at Wexford last month, the balance of this season's form has been respectable

runners.name: Teescomponentsyess
runners.spotlight: More than two years since his last win; has dropped to a 5lb lower mark now; gave signs of a revival when a remote third at Limerick on latest.
runners.comment: Now 5lb below his last winning mark, signs of a revival when a remote third on latest

runners.name: Opposite Ends
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; stablemate of Let Him B; well handicapped judged on last week's hurdle win at Clonmel; could be much too good for these rivals if getting a run.
runners.comment: Third reserve, stablemate of Let Him B, well handicapped judged on last week's hurdle win

runners.name: Sense The Tension
runners.spotlight: 0-11 in points; weak form in beginners' chases; transformation needed on handicap debut; that seems unlikely at the age of ten.
runners.comment: Transformation needed on handicap debut, that seems unlikely at the age of ten

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Hunters Chase

runners.name: Cold Old Fire
runners.spotlight: 2-12 in points; improved from Limerick run at Christmas when a 25-1 winner of a maiden hunter chase at Thurles in January; handled heavy conditions well, and did well to recover from a bad mistake at the seventh; third in the Tetratema at Gowran last time, winner Let's Go Champ franked the form with Aintree Foxhunter third.
runners.comment: Won at Thurles in January, form of Gowran third advertised in the Aintree Foxhunter

runners.name: Lawful Ruler
runners.spotlight: Only one win from ten starts in points; now on the upgrade; good run in defeat against West Of Idaho at Tyrella before a remote third in a hunter chase at Downpatrick; came good at Cork at fortnight ago, winning gamely with the two market leaders filling the places; stronger opposition now.
runners.comment: Won gamely at Cork with the two market leaders filling the places, stronger opposition now

runners.name: Bridge Of Machalee
runners.spotlight: Reserve; seven-race maiden in points; faces a huge task on racecourse debut.
runners.comment: Reserve, seven-race maiden in points, faces a huge task on racecourse debut

runners.name: Pirate Island
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden over hurdles; has won a point but was pulled up before two out in the hunter chase won by Lawful Ruler at Cork a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdler, pulled up before two out in the hunter chase won by Lawful Ruler at Cork

runners.name: Vodka Splash
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 11 starts in points; fair form recently but hard to fancy in this grade.
runners.comment: Only one win from 11 starts in points, fair form recently but hard to fancy in this grade

runners.name: Tinnock Force
runners.spotlight: Weak form in points, remote last of three finishers on latest; no apparent chance.
runners.comment: Weak form in points, remote last of three finishers on latest, no apparent chance

runners.name: Muskerry Rock
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on track for Mouse Morris and has a fair record in points, two wins and three places from six attempts; however, failed to make an impact at Limerick in December on his only hunter chase start.
runners.comment: Two wins/three places from six attempts in points, struggled on only hunter chase start

runners.name: I Walked The Line
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt in point-to-points for Cormac Doyle at Ballingarry (3m, good) last May; made a bright start for Willie and Patrick Mullins when beating Magic Sadler (winner of nine points) in a maiden hunter chase at Clonmel in January; 7lb claimer takes over now on a youngster expected to mature into a top-class performer in this category.
runners.comment: Looked promising when beating a decent yardstick at Clonmel, sustained progress is likely

runners.name: Catalani
runners.spotlight: Winner of three points and placed in a bumper; beaten when falling at the last in a hunter chase at Thurles in January; second in a point at Ballycrystal last month; has the benefit of a very valuable 7lb claim.
runners.comment: Beaten when falling at the last in a Thurles hunter chase, decent points form, top rider

runners.name: Sledgehammer
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a maiden point at Tyrella in February after two failures to complete; tailed off on hunter chase debut at Down Royal; made a bad mistake at the fourth and pulled up at the seventh at Downpatrick.
runners.comment: Tailed off on hunter chase debut at Down Royal, pulled up at the seventh at Downpatrick

runners.name: Roman Parish
runners.spotlight: 35-race maiden under rules; struggling in points since a maiden win last autumn.
runners.comment: 35-race maiden under rules, struggling in points since a maiden win last autumn

runners.name: Pure Decent
runners.spotlight: Not much mileage on the clock for a horse of his age; never in contention when a remote ninth of 13 finishers behind Lawful Ruler at Cork two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Never in contention when a remote ninth of 13 finishers behind Lawful Ruler at Cork

runners.name: Ta Se Rua
runners.spotlight: Veteran with modest strike rate in points (1-19); sprang a 33-1 shock in this race last year; likely to be available at big odds again; out of the money in his last two starts in points.
runners.comment: Sprang a 33-1 shock in this race last year, likely to be available at big odds again

races.race_name: Follow @AtTheRaces On X Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Sedgemoor
runners.spotlight: First-time blinkers saw him return to winning ways in a C&D classified event in February; won C&D handicap on second start for new yard last month; holds strong claims again.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins, the latest on second start for new yard last month.

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: Has been kept busy and won twice at Wolverhampton (6f) last month; suffered interference last time, which cost him any chance (usual cheekpieces absent), and holds claims with headgear back on.
runners.comment: Has been kept busy and won twice at Wolverhampton (6f) last month; hampered latest

runners.name: Gimmieminnie
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; sold for 2,600gns to join current yard in January; fair effort when fourth of 11 in a classified on stable debut at Wolverhampton (6f) last month and there may be some improvement in her.
runners.comment: Fair 4th in a classified on stable debut at Wolverhampton last month; may improve on that

runners.name: Bernie The Bear
runners.spotlight: All three wins came on AW in 2023 when trained by Richard Fahey; has run poorly on last two starts; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Has run poorly on last two starts; best watched for now.

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Belatedly opened her account at the 19th attempt in a classified over 5f here last month (first-time visor, retained since); hampered at the start when losing all chance last time; poor win record is off-putting.
runners.comment: Belatedly opened her account at the 19th attempt in a classified over 5f here last month.

runners.name: Asadjumeirah
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins but losing run is mounting up; needs a fast pace to aim at and is slipping down the weights; likely to come good again one of these days.
runners.comment: Has five wins on AW; losing run is mounting up but slipping down weights.

runners.name: Indy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over C&D in September 2024 off a 5lb higher mark; has deteriorated since and although now on a career-low mark she doesn't look ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: Sole success came over C&D in September 2024 off 5lb higher mark; has deteriorated since.

runners.name: Nazca
runners.spotlight: Three of his six wins have been over C&D but last one was back in March 2024; has been out of sorts on his last two starts.
runners.comment: Three of his six wins have been over C&D but last one was back in March 2024.

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning ways in a couple of classified events over C&D in February with cheekpieces back on; unable to land the three-timer back in a handicap over C&D last month; headgear left off here and looks vulnerable at the weights.
runners.comment: Landed a couple of classified events over C&D in February but held in a handicap since.

runners.name: World Of Darcy
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been on turf, rattling up a three-timer in October 2024; it's been downhill since and while cheekpieces replace usual blinkers, he makes little appeal here.
runners.comment: Downhill since he rattled up a three-timer in October 2024.

runners.name: Quercus
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in October 2024 and won twice at Catterick last summer; never showed when beating only one rival over C&D when last seen in November; best watched for new trainer after break.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024; two Catterick wins last summer; off since November; new trainer

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has form figures of 1221522 in handicaps and he's been beaten a short head and a nose in his two runs (1m4f/1m6f) this year; on same mark as last time and he should make another bold bid back in trip; major player.
runners.comment: Has record of 1221522 in handicaps including two close calls this year; major player

runners.name: Roger Henry
runners.spotlight: In good form for Gay Kelleway in second half of 2024 but he returned after a long absence with a heavy defeat on his stable debut here (1m) last month; back down 5lb but has plenty to prove and untried at this trip.
runners.comment: Back from long absence with a tailed-off effort here (1m) last month; others preferred

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Came good with 1m2f wins at Newcastle and Doncaster last month but he didn't fire when upped to 1m4f in his hat-trick bid at Lingfield last time; had a wide trip in that race but he needs to kick on again to make a big impact back on Tapeta.
runners.comment: Won here and at Doncaster last month but he didn't fire in his hat-trick bid at Lingfield

runners.name: Clansman
runners.spotlight: In good form since returning from seven months off in January including a win over C&D last month and close third at Southwell (1m6f) 13 days ago; ties in with Analogical on that latest form and he's respected back in trip.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month and was just behind Analogical at Southwell last time; respected
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Moon Sniper
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in the autumn and was a close third at Southwell (1m4f) on his penultimate run in January; left the Muir/Grassick stable for 17,000gns the following month; remains feasibly treated off only 1lb higher than for his last win and he needs a close look for new yard; tongue-tie reapplied.
runners.comment: C&D winner who changed hands for 17,000gns in February; market informative on stable debut

runners.name: Patrol
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated off 1lb higher at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) last April but he's not easy to predict and has finished down the field in his last four starts; may have needed his reappearance run at Wolverhampton (9.4f) last month but others are more convincing.
runners.comment: On workable mark but he's finished down the field in last four runs; others preferred

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mimi's Magic
runners.spotlight: Went close at Chelmsford (1m, AW) on her second 2yo run but she didn't fire at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in her final run last season; sets the standard on penultimate form but needs to get back on track after a break.
runners.comment: Sets standard on her best 2yo form but she ended last season with a disappointing run

runners.name: Ziata
runners.spotlight: 34,000gns yearling by Iffraaj; close fourth on Haydock debut (7f, good to soft) last September but she was a well-held third when odds-on at Nottingham (8.3f, good) the following month; that was behind a useful prospect but she has a bit to prove after six months off.
runners.comment: Turned over at 1-2 in final 2yo run and has a bit to prove after six months off

runners.name: Bintaziza
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to three winners including Flame Of Freedom (7f/1m including AW/KSA; RPR 87) and Little Bittern (6f AW; 60); dam 1m winner (74), closely related to 1m Listed winner Hen Night, half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 2 winner Amira's Prince; yard 2-9 in maidens this year and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 38,000euros yearling; yard 2-9 in maidens this year and she's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Astrid
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros foal; closely related to French winners Quickstep (7.5f/1m including AW/Listed; RPR 104) and Dave (Group-placed 1m/8.5f; 102), half-sister to three winners including Commander (Graded stakes-placed 5f-7f including 2yo; 102); dam ran once; plenty to like on paper; needs close look on debut.
runners.comment: 50,000euros foal; plenty to like on paper and she needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Ice Show
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns 2yo; third foal; dam 7f winner (RPR 98), half-sister to winners Queen Of Ice (1m4f Listed) and Gone Dutch (1m-1m3f), out of 1m2f Listed winner; hood is applied on debut and others are preferred.
runners.comment: 1,000gns 2yo; hood is applied on debut and others are preferred

runners.name: Sottna
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 1m2f winner Award Scheme (RPR 106), half-sister to three winners including Theme Park (7f/1m including AW; 91) and Tynwald (11.6f-1m5f; 83); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (78), closely related to 1m5f Listed winner; yard 1-34 in maidens last year and she could be one for later on.
runners.comment: Yard 1-34 in maidens last year and she could be one for later on

runners.name: Crown Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Time Test filly; third foal; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 89), sister to Swedish 1,000 Guineas/Norsk Derby winner High As A Kite, half-sister to useful 6.5f-1m winner London Protocol; yard 1-8 in maidens this year and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Time Test filly; yard 1-8 in maidens this year and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Clocker
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two runs in the autumn but she wasn't beaten far at Musselburgh (7f, soft) last time and that was encouraging from off the pace; open to more progress on this step back up in trip and she could be in the mix on her return.
runners.comment: Some promise in her final 2yo run and she's open to more progress back up in trip

runners.name: Luscious Lil
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two 1m novice events (Tapeta/good) last month, with a best RPR of 28.
runners.comment: Struggled in two 1m novice events (Tapeta/good) last month, with a best RPR of 28.

runners.name: The Bitters
runners.spotlight: Massive prices and she's finished tailed off over C&D and at Nottingham (1m2f, good) this spring.
runners.comment: Massive prices and she's finished tailed off over C&D and at Nottingham (1m2f) this spring

runners.name: Bridgefoot Rambler
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much at a big price in sole 2yo run and same story when last of six on recent stable debut at Thirsk (1m, good).
runners.comment: Has struggled at big prices in two runs on turf and the latest was on recent stable debut

runners.name: Beat The Odds
runners.spotlight: Big prices and she finished a tailed-off last in two runs last summer including over C&D; can only be watched on her comeback.
runners.comment: Big prices and she finished a tailed-off last in two runs last summer including over C&D

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gouken
runners.spotlight: The most experienced of these and has been placed on all five starts at 6f and 7f; has been gelded since his last run in October and he should go close.
runners.comment: The most experienced of these, having been placed on all five starts at 6f and 7f.

runners.name: Nebulon
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably when sixth on his debut at Windsor (4-1) last June; shaped like a winner in waiting when runner-up at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last month and he should take all the beating.
runners.comment: Shaped like a winner in waiting when runner-up at Wolverhampton (6f) last month.

runners.name: Slot
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when placed in two AW runs at 5f and 6f last year but was withdrawn after running loose before the start in two subsequent starts; she clearly has ability if it can be channelled.
runners.comment: Placed over 5f and 6f on AW as 2yo but ran loose and withdrawn in 2 subsequent attempts

runners.name: Kilfrush Desert
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut when third of 12 at Beverley (5f, good) last April; absent since and may need the run on this AW debut now up in trip.
runners.comment: Made a promising debut when third at Beverley last April but off since; now up in trip

runners.name: Alfa Impulsion
runners.spotlight: 34,000gns yearling; third foal; by Showcasing; half-sister to winners La Ferveur (French 8.5f; RPR 88) and Impelling (Czech 6.5f); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (94), half-sister to Italian 5f Listed winner Gamgoom; speedy pedigree but yard does best with handicappers so she may need time.
runners.comment: Has a speedy pedigree but it's possible she will need time

runners.name: April Diamond
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros yearling; sixth foal; by Make Believe; half-sister to winners Leodis Dream (prolific 5f including 2yo/AW; RPR 106), Pascalia (7f AW; 69) and Battle Point (5f AW; 68); dam 5f 2yo winner (73), half-sister to useful 7f-1m4f winner Original Choice; has work cut out on belated debut.
runners.comment: Has winning siblings at 5f-7f on the AW but has work cut out on belated debut

runners.name: Likeadyinwasp
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival on his debut at Southwell (5f, AW; 66-1) 11 days ago; much more needed now stepping up in trip.
runners.comment: Beat just one rival on his debut at Southwell (5f, AW; 66-1); much more needed for 6f

runners.name: Masai Angel
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on his debut at Hamilton (6f, good to soft; 12-1) last June; has been gelded since and has a lot to prove on AW debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off on his debut at Hamilton last June; gelded since; lots to prove on AW debut

runners.name: Lindoro
runners.spotlight: Ran to similar level in defeat at Doncaster (6f) and Redcar (7f) when last seen in the summer;  makes AW debut for new trainer and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Ran to similar level at Doncaster and Redcar last July; needs more for new trainer

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Territorial Star
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in all three qualifying runs (good to firm/Tapeta) and was placed again when solid third of 14 in Redcar nursery (6f, good to soft) in September; flopped in her final 2yo run but that was over 7f (raced freely) and she looks interesting back at this trip on her return.
runners.comment: Placed in first four 2yo runs and had an excuse over 7f last time; respected on her return

runners.name: Regal Dream
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has not progressed so far and was disappointing when favourite on her Catterick reappearance (5f handicap) 12 days ago; still unexposed and mark is falling but she needs more on AW debut.
runners.comment: Still unexposed and mark is falling but she needs more on AW debut

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but he went close in a C&D handicap last month and backed that up with another creditable third at Southwell (5f) last time; on unchanged mark and he's a big player if this sets up for his closing style.
runners.comment: 0-7 but he's finished a close third in last two starts including over C&D; key player

runners.name: O Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three qualifying runs last season but he returned after a gelding operation with an encouraging fourth on handicap debut at Catterick (5f, good) 12 days ago; 1lb lower here and has claims if he can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Solid fourth on handicap debut at Catterick and has claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Jane Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but she went close at Southwell (5f, Tapeta) last month and the step back up to 6f seemed to backfire at Wolverhampton last time; remains unexposed at this trip and has possibilities if she can recapture her penultimate form.
runners.comment: Went close at Southwell on penultimate run and has claims if she can recapture that form

runners.name: Fille Unique
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but was runner-up in a C&D maiden in the autumn and she ran well for a long way in a 6f handicap here (sixth of 13) last Monday; on the same mark as last time but she still has potential and could go well on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: 0-6 but she has form over C&D and could go well on this drop back in trip

runners.name: Nana's Boy George
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a great deal in three 2yo runs but he started his handicap career with a strong-finishing fourth at Wolverhampton (5f, Tapeta) last month; that was eye-catching from off the pace and he should benefit from that run; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Unexposed colt who was eye-catching fourth on handicap debut at Wolverhampton; dangerous

runners.name: Golden Havana
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a C&D nursery (blinkers added) in December and he ran into some trouble when well held over 6f here the following month; still has low mileage and he could be in the mix on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner on his penultimate run and he's in the mix back in trip after short break

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Headstrong sort who faded in two small-field nurseries over C&D towards the end of last year; still lightly raced but she needs improvement after 125 days off.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly but she needs improvement after 125 days off

runners.name: Bella Delizia
runners.spotlight: Improved form when justifying support with a brave win on nursery debut at Redcar (5f, good; 2-1) in October; 4lb higher on her return but she beat a clear second there and should have more to offer as a 3yo; respected back on AW.
runners.comment: Won on nursery debut at Redcar in October; respected off 4lb higher on her return

runners.name: Perfect Price
runners.spotlight: Her best effort in novice/maiden events last summer was a close fourth at Beverley (5f, good to firm) on her second start; now goes handicapping off what looks a workable mark and she needs watching in the market after 231 days off.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer who needs watching in the market after 231 days off

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Lord Capulet
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won three in a row over C&D at the start of the year; finished out the back behind Anthropologist here last month but he didn't lead in that race and raced freely after being crowded in the pack; this is another competitive event but he has possibilities if he can bounce back.
runners.comment: Three wins over C&D during the winter and he had an excuse here latest; not out of it

runners.name: Anthropologist
runners.spotlight: 4yo who recorded his third win when beating ten rivals over C&D last month; back up 4lb but he travelled strongly for a long way last time and hit a personal best with that stylish success; key player.
runners.comment: Won in good style over C&D last time and he's a big player again off 4lb higher

runners.name: Criminal Shore
runners.spotlight: Sole win was at Ripon last April but he's run plenty of good races since, including when fourth over C&D last month; continues to edge down the weights and he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Sole win was at Ripon last April but he's run plenty of good races since; in the mix

runners.name: King's School
runners.spotlight: Has a fine record over C&D including three wins, four seconds and four thirds; didn't fire here in his final run last year but he remains feasibly treated on his best form and his last win was after a break; needs watching in the market back from 139 days off.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner whose last win was after a break; needs watching in the market on return

runners.name: Phoenix Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-20 but his last win was over C&D last month and he was only beaten 2l behind Anthropologist here last time; remains feasibly treated on his best form and he has possibilities.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month and wasn't far behind Anthropologist here last time; possibilities

runners.name: Ziggy's Condor
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when winning here (6f, AW) in January but he has a mixed record since and was only sixth of nine at Chelmsford last month; this return to 7f is no problem but he needs to get back in the groove.
runners.comment: Won here in January but has mixed record since and he needs to get back in the groove

runners.name: Novak
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner who is generally reliable but he's not threatened in his last two starts, the latest behind Anthropologist over C&D last month; continues to edge down the weights but this is a deep race and he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Prolific winner but he's not threatened here in his last two starts; needs to raise game

runners.name: The Green Man
runners.spotlight: His last three wins have been at Newcastle and he produced a powerful finish when going close in a Class 4 here (6f) last month; looks interesting off only 1lb higher on this drop back in grade and his last success was over C&D.
runners.comment: His last three wins have been at Newcastle and he went close here last time; interesting
runners.owner: The Dalby Family And P Kirby

runners.name: Bobby Joe Leg
runners.spotlight: 11-time course winner who defied this mark over C&D in December; some creditable efforts since but he has been a bit up and down and was only sixth behind Anthropologist here last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: 11-time course winner but he was only sixth behind Anthropologist over C&D last time

runners.name: Call Me Betty
runners.spotlight: Two 7f wins on Tapeta last year including a dominant display in a C&D handicap in November; flopped here in her final run in 2025 but she's had a break since and it wouldn't be a surprise if she found more progress as a 4yo; needs a close look on her return.
runners.comment: Emphatic win over C&D on her penultimate run last year; needs a close look on return

runners.name: Kitaab
runners.spotlight: His last win was in October 2024 and he's been well held in most of his runs this year; on reduced mark but this looks tough back up in grade and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Both wins were in 2024 and this looks tough back up in trip and grade; down the list

runners.name: Too Much
runners.spotlight: Scored off this mark over C&D last March but she didn't hit the same level in seven subsequent runs last year; has a bit to prove after eight months off and regular hood is missing on this step back up in grade.
runners.comment: On last winning mark but she returns from eight months off and others are preferred

runners.name: Blazing Son
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner who scored off 1lb higher over C&D in September; has been hit and miss since but he finished second here (7f) on his penultimate run and wasn't disgraced after an awkward start at Southwell last time; usually races prominently and he's not ruled out back at this track.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark and was runner-up over C&D on his penultimate run; in the mix

runners.name: Cusack
runners.spotlight: Nine of his ten wins have come at this track (7f-1m2f) but last success was 13 months ago and he finished down the field here in his final two runs last year; looks opposable back from a break.
runners.comment: Course specialist but last win was 13 months ago and he has a bit to prove after a break

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Pebble Dash
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but he's been in good form on Tapeta recently including a close second here (7f) last month and a creditable fourth in another classified event at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last time; mark hasn't changed much and he's a key player back in a handicap.
runners.comment: 0-12 but he's been in good form on Tapeta recently and is a key player back in a handicap

runners.name: Bitacora
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who made the breakthrough in a classified event at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) last March but she's had only two runs since and has finished down the field in both of them; may have needed her comeback run at Southwell last month but she needs to bounce back near best; visor added.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton last March but she's struggled in both subsequent runs; visor added

runners.name: Esque Elegance
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 20 starts but she's had two seconds in C&D handicaps this year and was close up when sixth over 7f here (first-time visor) last time; should go well again back up in trip and she's in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-20 but she's had two seconds over C&D this year; in the mix

runners.name: Starshot
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 40 starts but he was runner-up over C&D last month and backed that up with a close fourth in another classified event here (7f) last time; nudged up another 1lb but he's an in-form contender who should go well again back up in trip.
runners.comment: Record of 1-40 but he's finished close up here (1m/7f) in his last two runs; respected

runners.name: Wheres The Crumpet
runners.spotlight: Both AW wins have been at Newcastle (1m/1m2f) but her last success was 11 months ago and she's been out of sorts in two runs this spring; down the list.
runners.comment: Her last success was 11 months ago and she's been out of sorts in two runs this spring

runners.name: Top Gun Tina
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Carlisle (1m) last July but her more recent form is less encouraging and she's finished down the field here (1m/7f) in last three starts; overall record is 1-31 and others are preferred; yard also runs Martin's Brig and Wheres The Crumpet.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-31 and she's finished down the field here in last three starts

runners.name: Take The A Train
runners.spotlight: Perked up with a second off a reduced mark over C&D last month but that was a five-runner race and he beat only one rival at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last time; record is now 0-9 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who finished out the back at Wolverhampton last time; others preferred

runners.name: Misemerald
runners.spotlight: Won two of her first three starts but beaten in her next 22 races; has dropped a long way in the weights but she struggled on her Doncaster reappearance last month and has plenty to prove back on AW.
runners.comment: Beaten in her last 22 starts and she has a lot to prove back on AW

runners.name: Tainted Love
runners.spotlight: Out of a Group-placed 6f-1m winner (including AW; RPR 100); struggled in three qualifying events over sprint trips this year but he now goes handicapping off a basement mark and is a possible improver on this step up to 1m; one to keep an eye on with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer who is a possible improver upped to 1m with headgear added

runners.name: Martin's Brig
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner but he's finished in rear in his last three runs and and his last win was 12 months ago; one of three runners for the yard and he needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Out of sorts in last three runs and he needs to turn things around; one of three for yard

runners.name: Teggy Lasso
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but he gave it a good shot from the front when a 2l fourth over C&D (tongue-tie added) last Monday; that was behind a resurgent rival who was well backed and this one still has potential as a handicapper; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 0-7 but was a creditable fourth over C&D last Monday and he still has potential; respected
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Wendy O'Leary

runners.name: Cloch Nua
runners.spotlight: Four wins in the early part of 2025 including three over C&D; down to a handy mark again but he generally struggled during the winter and was beaten 11l at Wolverhampton (7f) last time; has something to prove after a short break and he needs a major revival back up in trip.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner but he needs a major revival after a short break

runners.name: Ridgemaster
runners.spotlight: Struggled in six runs for Hugo Palmer but he sprang a surprise at 66-1 when scoring on his stable debut here (7f) in December; that was a convincing success but he struggled back here the following month and has something to prove again after that 11l defeat.
runners.comment: 66-1 win here (7f) on stable debut in December but he was nowhere near that form next time

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: 12yo who can miss the break but his last five wins have been at Newcastle and latest was over C&D in January; hasn't matched that form since but he's now 1lb lower than for his last success and he should get a good tow into this race; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 13-time winner and latest was off 1lb higher over C&D in January; not ruled out

races.race_name: Adam Scott Celebratory Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Smoke Trail
runners.spotlight: Yet to make any significant further progress since making all in 14-runner course maiden (2m, good to soft) on rules debut in October but wasn't beaten far when fourth of six in Newbury handicap (2m, good to soft) in February and ought to play a significant role in this.
runners.comment: Yet to progress from course win in October but still has a big role to play

runners.name: Changemyluck
runners.spotlight: Improved mare who has finished second here (2m, good to soft) on her last two appearances; it's quite tricky to gauge what she achieved behind 1-10 favourite on first occasion or behind 300-1 stablemate last time but, nevertheless, she's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Runner-up here on last two hurdling starts; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Herja
runners.spotlight: Safely held in two chases last month but consistently showed a fair level of form over hurdles in 2025, gaining her win in Sedgefield maiden (2m4f, good) in September; big player back in this sphere; hood left off today.
runners.comment: Safely held in two chases in March but consistent at a fair level over hurdles in 2025

runners.name: Nobotheratall
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when making all in good-ground Carlisle bumper in October; behind two of today's rivals when weakening seventh on hurdle debut here (2m, good to soft) this month but ran well for a long way and wasn't beaten far; can feature if that outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; ran well for a long way on hurdle debut and might come on for the outing

runners.name: Hashtag Queen
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when 250-1 for last month's hurdling debut at Wetherby (2m, good).
runners.comment: Tailed off when 250-1 for last month's hurdling debut at Wetherby (2m, good)

runners.name: Bogha Baiste
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on hurdle debut in January but placed twice at big odds since, more recently when 80-1 third (just behind Changemyluck) in race won by 300-1 outsider here (2m, good to soft) this month; can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Pulled up on hurdling debut but placed twice at big odds since; could have a say
runners.owner: A Lee, J Matterson, D Mullen & Partner

runners.name: Thorneylands
runners.spotlight: Close second in Newcastle bumper (good to soft) in December and displayed clear promise in two Carlisle novice hurdles (both 2m1f, good to soft) this year, albeit he was pulled up on run-in after losing his action last time; others have more compelling claims here, though.
runners.comment: Close second in a bumper and also showed clear promise on both hurdling starts

runners.name: The Grey Venture
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who bettered his inauspicious rules debut when 66-1 fourth at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) this month; needs to take another good step forward here, though; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Second hurdle run was much better than the first; another step forward is needed, though

runners.name: Melbourne Ghost
runners.spotlight: Not beaten that far when fifth of seven here (2m5f, good to soft) last month, his second hurdle start, but looked unenthusiastic and needs significant improvement to feature today.
runners.comment: Not beaten that far when fifth here last month but needs significant improvement today

races.race_name: Malcolm "Scarra" Scott Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Kilmore Rock
runners.spotlight: 4-12 over hurdles (including C&D win); out of sorts when pulled up twice in the winter (wore cheekpieces) but bounced back with creditable third in valuable series final at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft; first-time blinkers) last month and has possibilities if in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Bounced back in new blinkers with creditable third in series final at Musselburgh in March

runners.name: Heart Above
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (2m2f/2m5f) in the autumn; not in same form lately and untested over this far but back on a good mark and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Not at best lately but won twice here in the autumn and is back on a good mark

runners.name: Starlyte
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner, most recently in valuable series final (2m5f, good to soft) in March 2025; generally disappointing this season but kept on well for huge-odds fourth in mares' Listed race here (3m, good to soft) last month and returns to a handicap on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; back in a handicap after huge-odds Listed fourth here last month

runners.name: Jehol De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Came good at sixth attempt, keeping on resolutely at Newcastle (2m6f, good to soft) three weeks ago; 6lb higher here but has plenty of scope for further progress if his jumping is more fluent this time.
runners.comment: Came good at Newcastle last month, despite some poor jumping; can improve again

runners.name: Dollar Collar
runners.spotlight: Now 1lb lower than when winning this race in 2025 (good ground) but fell in a chase last month and dropped out of contention rather tamely when back over hurdles here (2m5f, good; breathing issue reported) 18 days ago; might be aided by refitted tongue-tie but more consistent rivals appeal more.
runners.comment: Won this off 1lb higher in 2025 but more consistent rivals appeal more

runners.name: Nosy Neighbour
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who kept on for clear second behind Jehol De Thaix on last month's handicap debut at Newcastle (2m6f, good to soft); big player if first-time cheekpieces prompt any further progress.
runners.comment: Kept on for second behind Jehol De Thaix on last month's handicap debut; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Jericoacoara
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his French hurdle win in 2023 but was placed on both starts this year, more recently when close second in valuable series final here (2m5f, good to soft) last month; must be considered.
runners.comment: Close second in valuable series final here (2m5f) last month; must be considered
runners.owner: G R Orchard & Mrs J M Gray

runners.name: Rauzan
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with creditable third (just behind Jericoacoara) in valuable series final here (2m5f, good to soft) last month and also ran well when second in small field at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) 16 days ago; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Back in good form lately, more notably when placed in valuable series final here (2m5f)

runners.name: Secret Secret
runners.spotlight: Won here in 2023 but probably better known as a Cartmel regular now (gained last two wins there); off since a poor run in November and might be of greater interest when back at Cartmel and with this outing behind him.
runners.comment: Absent since poor run in November and tends to save his best for Cartmel now

runners.name: Menaggio
runners.spotlight: Seemed to be travelling well before falling four out at Southwell (2m4f, good; first run after wind op) last month and remains well handicapped but has not been firing on all cylinders for new stable this season; swaps visor for first-time cheekpieces; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Below best this season, albeit seemed to be going well before falling last time

runners.name: Magic Wave
runners.spotlight: Course winner (2m5f, good) a year ago; absent since disappointing run in January but his C&D (good to soft) second just after Christmas was a good effort and he'll be right in the thick of things if back on song today.
runners.comment: Off since poor run in January but was good second over C&D just after Christmas

races.race_name: Royal Caledonian Hunt Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Looking Splendid
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with career-best performance this month, drawing clear to win by 11l over C&D (good) on chasing debut; 9lb higher in a higher-grade race today but could easily have more to offer over fences.
runners.comment: Won by 11l over C&D on recent chase debut; 9lb higher in higher-grade race today

runners.name: Old Gregorian
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner over hurdles; finished alone in a match race on seasonal/chase debut in October then wasn't beaten far when last of four over C&D (good to soft) in February; eased in grade today and could still have potential over fences.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when last of four over C&D in February; still unexposed over fences

runners.name: Breizh River
runners.spotlight: Made all to land good prize at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) in January and is also very well suited by this track but he's no longer the force of old and was unable to land a telling blow at Carlisle last month; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Landed good prize in January but is not the force of old; others appeal more

runners.name: Joecooker
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over fences but gave very good account when third in valuable series final over C&D (good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) last month and has been dropped 1lb since; big player if new headgear continues to help.
runners.comment: 0-6 over fences but creditable third in valuable C&D series final last month; big player

runners.name: Kay Tara Tara
runners.spotlight: Safely held on last month's chasing debut at Ludlow (2m4f, good) but that came after a 489-day absence and she's well handicapped on old hurdling form; no surprise if she gets closer to the target today.
runners.comment: Safely held on last month's chasing debut but that came after a long absence

runners.name: Garde Des Champs
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 10yo who belatedly opened his chasing account at Perth (2m, good; first-time cheekpieces) last summer; absent since below-par effort in October but ran well when fresh here (2m4f, good) last spring and returns from latest break on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Largely consistent 10yo; off since October but ran well when fresh last spring; a possible

runners.name: Special Rate
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his four chase wins for Philip Kirby during 2023-24 season but has made the frame on all four starts for current stable since December, most recently when third of five at Newcastle (2m4f, good) nine days ago; tried in cheekpieces today; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Without a win since 2024 but consistent for new stable since December; in the mix again

runners.name: Jet Legs
runners.spotlight: Won off today's mark at Hexham (2m4f, good to soft; first-time visor) towards end of last season but is yet to build upon his pleasing reappearance run in October and needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Yet to build upon pleasing reappearance run in October and needs to up his game
runners.owner: Mrs Suzy Brown & Peter R Brown

races.race_name: Reg And Betty Tweedie Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dare To Shout
runners.spotlight: Not really firing on all cylinders since scrambling home by a neck on seasonal debut at Carlisle (3m, good) in October but now 6lb below that mark, and didn't run badly when last of five finishers here (3m2f, good to soft) in March; had another wind op (his third) soon after that latest outing; one to consider.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly when fifth here (3m2f) last month and has become well handicapped

runners.name: Walk On Quest
runners.spotlight: Last season's chasing four-timer included two course wins (2m1f/2m5f) but he's been disappointing since, barring a keeping-on second at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in December; pulled up in Carlisle series final won by Wolfburg last month, and moves back up in trip with questions to answer.
runners.comment: Four wins last season (two here) but largely disappointing since; has a point to prove

runners.name: Cadell
runners.spotlight: Made all in first-time blinkers over C&D (good to soft) in November, his third course win, but was pulled up on all three subsequent outings and needs switch to first-time visor to provoke another resurgence.
runners.comment: Made all in new blinkers over C&D in November but pulled up three times since; now visored

runners.name: Wolfburg
runners.spotlight: Readily drew clear to win valuable series final by 9l at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft) last month and is now 2-5 over fences; went up 11lb for that but could have untapped potential over this longer trip; respected.
runners.comment: Won series final by 9l at Carlisle last month and is now 2-5 over fences; respected

runners.name: Red Happy
runners.spotlight: In excellent form in February/March 2025, winning three in a row at around 3m (good to soft/good), but has fitness to prove after 393-day absence and will need to hit the ground running to have any chance off current mark.
runners.comment: Won three in a row in February/March 2025 but this is his first run since

runners.name: Erne River
runners.spotlight: Ran well for a long way when fourth at Doncaster (3m, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) in February and remains well handicapped but this steadily regressive 11yo has become inconsistent, and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way at Doncaster last time but this 11yo has become inconsistent

races.race_name: Scottish Racing Academy Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Gonnino
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles and has rather mixed record this season but ran big race in defeat at Carlisle (2m3f, good to soft) 16 days ago and has obvious claims if in the right mood again here.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but ran big race at Carlisle this month; player if judged on that

runners.name: Alvesta
runners.spotlight: 0-22 on the Flat and 0-7 over hurdles; sold out of Matt Crawley's stable for 3,400gns after well-held fourth at Fakenham (2m, soft) in February but ought to be better suited by the forecast faster ground here, and could plausibly have been perked up by her new regime.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles but could plausibly have been perked up by recent stable switch

runners.name: Ellistrin Bay
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only five hurdle runs but was pulled up on last month's handicap debut, having refused to settle; might be more relaxed without blinkers (worn for last three races) here but still looks too risky.
runners.comment: Pulled up on last month's handicap debut, having refused to settle; blinkers discarded

runners.name: Highly Recommended
runners.spotlight: Still has low mileage and will be suited by today's forecast good ground but, following a long absence, has been pulled up on both outings this year; needs a good boost from the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Pulled up on both outings this year, after a long layoff; cheekpieces added today

runners.name: Parisian Fashion
runners.spotlight: Absent since ending 2025 with two disappointing efforts but resumes on a good mark and has fairly good record when fresh; could be dangerous if first-time visor has desired effect.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 out of form but resumes on a good mark and has fairly good record when fresh

runners.name: Big Love
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who is likely to have entered a few notebooks after last month's handicap debut at Wetherby (2m, good to soft), where he kept on steadily from the rear for a never-dangerous fourth; moves up in trip here; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Kept on steadily for never-dangerous fourth on handicap debut, and still has potential

runners.name: Struth
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise in three novice/maiden hurdles (1m7f-2m) last September-November but was useful on the Flat in his prime and may yet transfer more of that ability to this code; wears first-time tongue-tie for handicap hurdle debut; market may guide.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in novice/maiden hurdles but was useful on the Flat; interesting

runners.name: Gozo
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who won a juvenile event on hurdling debut in 2023 but was not seen again over jumps until pulled up in Catterick handicap on stable debut in January; current ability in this sphere remains hard to gauge.
runners.comment: Fair Flat racer; pulled up when returned to hurdling for stable debut in January; risky

runners.name: Ozzy Cosmo
runners.spotlight: Disappointing 7yo who has been soundly beaten in three chases this year and now reverts to hurdling; usual cheekpieces left off today; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Disappointing 7yo who has been soundly beaten in three chases this year; now back hurdling

runners.name: Interchangeable
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two respectable efforts at Carlisle (2m1f/2m3f, soft) but was soundly beaten there on latest outing in February (raced too freely) and has now been unplaced all four starts for current stable; sports refitted hood and first-time tongue-tie here; others look safer.
runners.comment: Unplaced all four starts for new stable this season; others look safer

races.race_name: Scottish Racing Academy Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Rememberthename
runners.spotlight: Placed in five of his eight runs over hurdles without looking particularly progressive; in good heart though and looks sure to win a race such as this; place claims at least.
runners.comment: Placed in five of his eight runs over hurdles without looking particularly progressive.

runners.name: Newport
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; got off the mark over hurdles at Musselburgh (1m7f, soft) in December off 6lb lower; placed three times since, running up to his best when second of 11 finishers at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last month; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles at Musselburgh in December off 6lb lower; placed three times since.

runners.name: Glenridding
runners.spotlight: 1m Flat winner in September 2024 when trained by the Kublers; yet to make the frame in four starts over hurdles but has time on his side; hood worn on last two starts is left off; may do better at some point.
runners.comment: Yet to make the frame in four starts over hurdles but has time on his side.

runners.name: Hunters Spring
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner who has made the frame on all of her three starts over hurdles; steps up in trip on her handicap debut and should give a good account provided she shows improvement.
runners.comment: Good-ground bumper winner who has made the frame on all of her three starts over hurdles.

runners.name: Miss Friday Lions
runners.spotlight: 0-9 under rules; jumping was less than fluent when beaten 52l at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) last month; can do better in time.
runners.comment: Jumping was less than fluent when beaten 52l at Newcastle last month.

runners.name: Barrakilla Vage
runners.spotlight: 0-8 under rules; pulled up when 80-1 on handicap debut at Hexham (2m, soft) last month; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 80-1 on handicap debut at Hexham last month.

runners.name: Chosen Hero
runners.spotlight: Sole success over hurdles came in January 2024 when trained by Archie Watson; she has offered little in three runs for current yard and it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: She has offered little in three runs for current yard and it's easy to look elsewhere.

runners.name: Lester Mail
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper here last April; has shown ability in three runs over hurdles and can be expected to show improvement now that he steps up in trip on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won a bumper here last April; has shown ability in three runs over hurdles.

runners.name: Fine Point
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Kempton last April off 2lb higher when trained by Nigel Hawke; ran as if he needed the run when fourth of six finishers at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) 19 days ago on his stable debut after eight months off; sports first-time cheekpieces and has to be of interest.
runners.comment: Ran as if he needed the run when fourth at Sedgefield 19 days ago after eight months off.

races.race_name: Church House Investments Buccleuch Cup (A Maiden Hunters' Chase) (Corinthian Spirit Qualifier)

runners.name: Theoldboggroad
runners.spotlight: Third in this race last year, his only start under rules, and returns here after winning an open point at Overton (3m, good) last month; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Placed in this race in 2025, and returns here after open point win last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Amma Lord
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner; gave good account when fourth (just behind Theoldboggroad) in this race a year ago but has not been at his best in points this season and needs help from the new cheekpieces.
