runners.comment: Fourth in this last year but not at best in points this season; new cheekpieces

runners.name: Flying Skipper
runners.spotlight: Made all to fend off The Back Braes in maiden point at Friars Haugh (3m, good to soft) last month, his fifth attempt, but was about 12l behind Boley Bob when third at Tranwell (3m, good to soft) three weeks later; further progress required.
runners.comment: Maiden point winner; about 12l behind Boley Bob when third on latest outing

runners.name: Phantoms Cave
runners.spotlight: Two point wins this year, the latter gained easily by 16l in Overton conditions race (2m4f, good) last month; ought to make his presence felt on rules debut.
runners.comment: Easy winner of conditions point at Overton last month; makes rules debut today

runners.name: Boley Bob
runners.spotlight: 2-4 in Irish points in 2025 and 4-5 in British points for new connections this year, the latest win gained in three-runner contest this month; seems versatile regarding ground and is a likely contender on rules debut.
runners.comment: 2-4 in Irish points and 4-5 in British points; likely contender on rules debut

runners.name: Bentley Road
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer who was pulled up in 2024 edition of this race on only previous rules start; ran well in two 2m4f maiden points last month (albeit ran out at final fence on first occasion) but others look stronger.
runners.comment: Ran well in two maiden points last month but others look stronger here

runners.name: The Back Braes
runners.spotlight: 0-7 under all codes but returned from long layoff with close/clear second behind Flying Skipper in Friars Haugh point (3m, good to soft) for new trainer last month, and has possibilities here if that run brought him on.
runners.comment: Returned from long absence with point second last month and might come on for the run

runners.name: High On The Hill
runners.spotlight: Began year with two point wins at Alnwick (maiden/restricted) and again ran well when second (beaten about 4l) behind Boley Bob at Tranwell (3m, good to soft) last month; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Dual point winner this year; again ran well when second to Boley Bob at Tranwell last time

races.race_name: Racing TV Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dilrisk
runners.spotlight: Beaten 4l when fourth behind Midday Rendezvous at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last month when favourite; looks sure to improve and must come into the reckoning.
runners.comment: Beaten 4l when fourth behind Midday Rendezvous at Newcastle last month when favourite.

runners.name: Cape Warrior
runners.spotlight: Turned in a creditable effort when runner-up on his debut at Wetherby (2m, good) last month; difficult to gauge what that form is worth but he should be competitive.
runners.comment: Turned in a creditable effort when runner-up on his debut at Wetherby last month.

runners.name: Teescomponents Gal
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to winners Fenland Tiger (2m4f-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Teescomponentstrig (3m hurdle); dam placed bumper/2m5f hurdle, half-sister to bumper winner Seren Rouge, out of 1m3f Flat and useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner; pedigree is laden with stamina and unlikely to be seen at her best until she steps up in trip over hurdles.
runners.comment: Unlikely to be seen at her best until she steps up in trip over hurdles.

runners.name: Eagle Warrior
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer; fifth behind Midday Rendezvous at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last month; wasn't beaten far and any significant improvement should see him bang there.
runners.comment: Fifth behind Midday Rendezvous at Newcastle last month.

runners.name: Yorgunnarock
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam once-raced sister to bumper winner Nana Joan, half-sister to bumper and 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Glencassley, out of 2m/2m3f hurdle winning half-sister to Velka Pardubicka winner Registana; yard not renowned for their exploits in bumpers and he's likely to need more time.
runners.comment: Yard not renowned for their exploits in bumpers and he's likely to need more time.

runners.name: Midday Rendezvous
runners.spotlight: 9-1, made a winning debut in 12-runner bumper at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last month, overcoming greenness to get up on the post; saddled with a 7lb penalty but has more to offer for yard with fine bumper record this year; player.
runners.comment: 9-1, made a winning debut in 12-runner bumper at Newcastle last month.

races.race_name: Flat Is Back On Racing TV EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Miss Lizzy
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when seventh of eight, beaten just under 9l, on debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) a fortnight ago; plenty of improvement is needed but she's the only one of these with experience and it will be no surprise if she takes a big step forward for Newmarket yard; a tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: Plenty of improvement needed on second start but she's the only one with experience

runners.name: Angel Footsteps
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 5f winner (RPR 78), half-sister to 6f winner, out of 5f/6f winning half-sister to Irish Derby winner Westover; in-form trainer is 4-21 (19%) with 2yos at Redcar and this Harry Angel debutante could have a part to play.
runners.comment: In-form trainer is 4-21 (19%) with 2yos at Redcar; could have a part to play on debut
runners.owner: Asgard Thoroughbred Racing I & Les Boyer

runners.name: Arcadian Days
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns yearling; by Lope Y Fernandez; first foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 71), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Rocket Rodney, out of once-raced sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Mister Cosmi; trainer's horses are running well and he can ready one first time; monitor the betting on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer's horses are running well and he can ready one first time; monitor the betting

runners.name: Cailin Aine
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; third foal; Invincible Army half-sister to Norwegian 4.5f-7f winner Final Angel; unraced dam from talented family; one of two for the yard; likely to improve for this debut experience.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Norwegian 4.5f-7f winner; likely to improve for this debut experience

runners.name: Cheeky Chesca
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Invincible Army sister to Bahraini 6f winner, half-sister to four winners including My Mate Alfie (6f/7f including 2yo/Group 3), Shameful (6f 2yo; RPR 94) and Wiseacre (1m AW; 83); dam 5f 2yo Group 3 winner; trainer runs two; she might need this debut outing.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling with potential in her pedigree; she might need this debut outing

runners.name: Bea Kindly
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 92), half-sister to winners Brian Ryan (German 7f Group 3) and Shark Two One (6f/7f including 2yo; 97), out of 6f 2yo winner; this Caturra debutante may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Third foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 92); he may be one for further down the line

runners.name: State Of Gold
runners.spotlight: 12,000euros foal, £20,000 yearling; third foal; State Of Rest half-sister to French Listed-placed 6.5f/7f winner Summer Sail; dam unraced half-sister to 6f-1m winner, out of Listed-placed 7f/1m winner; trainer's sole 2yo runner this year showed promise at 33-1; could give a good account.
runners.comment: Half-sister to French Listed-placed 6.5f/7f winner Summer Sail; could give a good account

runners.name: Wopbopaloomop
runners.spotlight: 21,000gns yearling; third foal; closely related to 6f winner Candy (including 2yo Listed); dam unplaced 6f, related to two very talented sprinters; Newmarket trainer is 2-5 with 2yos here in the last five years; this Ubettabelieveit newcomer is one to be interested in.
runners.comment: 21,000gns yearling; closely related to 6f 2yo Listed winner Candy; one to be interested in

runners.name: Vollering
runners.spotlight: £58,000 yearling; second foal; dam placed 7f 2yo (RPR 68), half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Salouen; Lambourn trainer is 5-10 with 2yos here in the last five years and this Sioux Nation newcomer is a strong contender.
runners.comment: £58,000 yearling; Lambourn trainer is 5-10 with 2yos here in the last five years

races.race_name: Better Value With Racecourse Bookmakers Handicap

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: She's dropped down the weights and hasn't run badly on her two starts this year (7f, AW); however, she's 0-9 and needs something extra on this first crack at 1m.
runners.comment: Hasn't been running badly this year but 0-9 and needs something extra today

runners.name: Worlington
runners.spotlight: Struggled towards end of last year on first three starts but gelded subsequently and was an entirely different proposition when 7-2 runner-up on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) four weeks ago; still didn't appear to be the finished article and is open to further improvement; turf debut.
runners.comment: Still didn't appear to be the finished article when 7-2 runner-up on handicap debut

runners.name: Amazing Anita
runners.spotlight: Encouraging third on reappearance at Southwell (1m, AW) last month; failed to build on that at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) but there's a change of headgear today and she's not written off for her respected Newmarket yard.
runners.comment: Encouraging third on reappearance; failed to build on it last time but not written off

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: Beaten just a neck over C&D (good to soft) last September but tailed off at 28-1 on reappearance at Ripon (1m, good to soft) only last Thursday to make it 0-8; very hard to fancy on the back of that.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D last September but tailed off on reappearance only last Thursday

runners.name: Hear The Drums
runners.spotlight: Improved to win at 11-2 at Leicester (7f, soft) last October on his second nursery start; gelded since; he's on quicker ground for this reappearance but his pedigree provides optimism that he has more to offer.
runners.comment: Won on final 2yo start and pedigree provides optimism that he has more to offer

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 15 starts but that was over C&D (good to soft) last September when 3lb higher; also effective on fast ground; reappeared with respectable fifth at Southwell (1m, AW) recently and could show the benefit of that outing this afternoon; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Just one win from her 15 starts but that was over C&D when 3lb higher; not ruled out

runners.name: Princess Coco
runners.spotlight: There are winners in her pedigree and she makes her handicap debut off a basement mark; a market check is advised but she's achieved little across her four races and improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Makes her handicap debut off a basement mark but she's achieved little thus far

runners.name: Battenburg Belle
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for close third of 14 at 14-1 on handicap debut at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) recently, behind two who got first run; there's every chance that 1m will be within range and she could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Kept on well for third over 6f on handicap debut and every chance that 1m is within range

runners.name: Resdev Time
runners.spotlight: She's shown ability and returns from some time off on a reduced mark; however, she needs to produce more than she managed on her three nursery starts last October/September; the blinkers are left off.
runners.comment: She's shown ability but is 0-6 and needs to raise her game following some time off

runners.name: Wild Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Down the field last April-June on first three starts (5f/6f) and absent since (gelded in interim); however, his pedigree suggests that he will be suited by the step up in trip on this handicap debut and it will be no surprise to see a big performance, especially if well backed.
runners.comment: Returns from absence but the new trip could suit on handicap debut; monitor the betting

runners.name: Dalamara
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her 6f maiden/novice form to win at 16-1 on nursery debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in December; bred to do better still and returns to action with her trainer among the winners; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in December on sole handicap start and she's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Itszaboy
runners.spotlight: Similar form when soundly beaten in fifth/seventh here last October on his first three starts (7f/1m, good to soft/good); not obviously well treated off opening mark but he's one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: No threat here last October on first three starts; one to watch in betting on h'cap debut

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV In Stunning HD Handicap

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his 22 starts but he often ran well last year and did likewise when third at Thirsk (1m, good) ten days ago on his reappearance; went close over this C&D last May (good to firm); can be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but often runs well, as when third at Thirsk on recent reappearance

runners.name: Elettaria
runners.spotlight: Poor 1-22 strike-rate on turf and not at the top of her game on Southwell AW on her last two starts, but she's never really shone there; she's 5lb lower than for her close fourth over C&D (good) last October and is an interesting contender now away from Southwell.
runners.comment: Below par at Southwell last month but that's not her track; interesting off a handy mark

runners.name: One Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over 7f but has frequently hinted at 6f that this trip will suit, as when keeping on for 2l fourth at Catterick (good) on recent reappearance; she has a solid record here and could be thereabouts.
runners.comment: 0-6 over 7f but has frequently hinted at 6f that this trip will suit, latest 12 days ago

runners.name: Woodrafff
runners.spotlight: Tends to be slowly away and is the type who needs everything to drop right if he's to win, but he's run several good races on AW this year and was fourth of 14 back on turf at Thirsk (7f, good) recently; in the mix.
runners.comment: Several good races on AW this year and another back on turf recently; in the mix

runners.name: Little Ted
runners.spotlight: 9yo who won at Leicester (1m, good to firm) last June and is on a competitive mark on the pick of this winter's AW form; however, he was below par in January when last seen and needs to bounce back following a break.
runners.comment: 9yo who is on a competitive mark but was below par on his latest outing in January

runners.name: Tickets
runners.spotlight: Often slowly away and frequently fails to settle; however, he won here (6f, good to firm) last April and got back on the scoresheet at Newcastle (6f, AW) in February; good second at Newcastle (6f) last time; in good form at Newcastle in autumn 2024 when he last tackled 7f and he's in with a chance.
runners.comment: Running well over 6f; in good form in autumn 2024 when he last tackled 7f

runners.name: Flying Star
runners.spotlight: She's shown ability and has dropped to a basement mark; however, her slow starts tend to be costly and she's 0-12; comes with risks attached on this first outing since last August.
runners.comment: She's shown ability and has dropped to a basement mark, but 0-12 and tends to start slowly

runners.name: She's A Goldigger
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who is unraced on fast ground; however, she ran a couple of promising races last autumn, including here, and reappears with her yard going well; one of two for stable; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden but some promising efforts last autumn and she's not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Mayo County
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark over C&D (good to firm) last May and two positive performances in defeat this winter; however, she's hasn't been at her best on her last two runs and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: C&D winner; some good form this winter but below best on her last two runs

runners.name: Looks Fantastic
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but returned from a break with an encouraging fourth at Southwell (1m, AW) recently; also effective on turf; might not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-10 but encouraging recent run, following a break, and might not be far away

runners.name: Angel Of England
runners.spotlight: Went close at Newcastle (6f, AW) in December but well beaten on last two starts, most recently at 50-1 at Thirsk ten days ago following wind surgery; his long losing run continues and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Thirsk recently following wind surgery and his long losing run continues
runners.owner: The Jocks And The Geordies II

runners.name: Without Delay
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten last November on last two starts but she returns having had wind surgery and is 3lb lower than when winning at Catterick (7f, good to firm) last July; not discounted.
runners.comment: Reappears having had wind surgery and is 3lb below last winning mark; not discounted

runners.name: Electric Lightning
runners.spotlight: Well beaten last October when last seen but on heavy ground; prior to that he was a close fourth over C&D and this lightly raced 5yo reappears with his trainer (who runs two) among the winners; could be involved.
runners.comment: Effective over C&D and this lightly raced 5yo reappears with his trainer among the winners

runners.name: Far Ahead
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who is very well handicapped on his peak turf efforts last May-August; his four heavy defeats in the winter were on AW but he returns to turf after a break with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: He's tumbled down the weights but suffered four heavy defeats on AW in the winter
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

races.race_name: racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Cadarn
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in mid-division on last month's stable/seasonal debut in the Spring Mile at Doncaster but that was his first encounter with slow ground and he may have needed the run; won twice over this C&D (good to firm) last spring, including this race, and could play a leading role back here.
runners.comment: Could improve for stable/seasonal debut at Doncaster and he's 2-2 here

runners.name: Sea Legend
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Newcastle (1m, AW) this year, including on his latest start last month; 2lb out of the handicap today and he's 0-13 on turf, but has been placed a few times on grass and is not discounted.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year; 0-13 on turf but placed a few times and he's not discounted

runners.name: Seagolazo
runners.spotlight: 0-7 last season and inconsistent; however, he was a close third of 11 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last July and also showed spark when third at Southwell (1m4f, AW) in September; on a career-low mark and not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-7 last season and inconsistent but showed spark on occasions and he's not ruled out
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Jolly Roger
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has been running well in UAE this year, going close in big-field handicap in February (1m, good) then third of ten last month (1m, dirt); major player if continuing the good work back in Britain.
runners.comment: Running well in UAE this year; major player if continuing the good work back in Britain

runners.name: Double Parked
runners.spotlight: This C&D brought out the best in him last season, with two wins in the summer (good to soft/good to firm) and a close third of 13 in October when last seen; strong contender if fully tuned up for this reappearance.
runners.comment: This C&D brought out the best in him last season; strong contender if fully tuned up

runners.name: Skipper
runners.spotlight: Failed to shine in France last summer on his last two starts; however, he has potential off this mark if tapping back into the promise shown in Britain in 2024 at the start of his career; the betting could be informative on his first run for Hamad Al Jehani (remains in same ownership).
runners.comment: Below par in France last summer; potential off this mark if tapping back into 2024 promise

runners.name: Intrusively
runners.spotlight: Third in 2yo Group 2 in 2024; unable to reproduce that promise last season and well beaten in last month's Spring Mile on stable/seasonal debut; however, he has a reduced mark to work with and his new trainer may well find the key to him sooner rather than later; the returning visor may be a plus.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but new trainer may well find the key sooner rather than later

runners.name: Diderot
runners.spotlight: Very well handicapped on AW form up to 2024 but inferior on turf (0-6); down the field in January (7f, AW) following an absence and refused to race in February; arrives with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Better on AW than turf and he refused to race in February last time; plenty to prove

runners.name: New Image
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his form in 2024, when he won four times; not written off for good yard and one to watch in the betting after a break, but he ran just twice last year and was well beaten each time; something to prove.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on 2024 form but ran just twice last year and well beaten each time

races.race_name: Watch Live On Racing TV Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Mr Colonel
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck on his first two runs last year before excelling himself to finish fifth in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) in October; should go close if ready to roll.
runners.comment: Excelled himself to finish fifth in a Group 3 at Newmarket in October.

runners.name: Bay Of Myths
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last month, beating a Godolphin-owned 3yo; carries a 7lb penalty but should go close on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at Chelmsford last month, beating a Godolphin-owned 3yo.

runners.name: Heathers Feather
runners.spotlight: Beaten over 14l on debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) last Monday; hard to fancy on turf debut.
runners.comment: Beaten over 14l on debut at Newcastle last Monday; hard to fancy.

runners.name: According To Mark
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well after being slowly away to finish fourth on his debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in November; will know more this time and should go well on this step up in trip on turf debut.
runners.comment: Stayed on well to finish fourth on his debut at Newcastle in November.

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Mafting
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden over C&D (good to firm) last May but subsequently didn't build on that promise in handicaps; arrives here on a career-low mark and needs to rediscover his earlier form.
runners.comment: Won a maiden over C&D last May but subsequently didn't build on that promise in handicaps.

runners.name: Hill O Rue
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with his second win of the campaign at Beverley (1m2f, soft) in September; yet to race on ground quicker than good but his progressive profile gives him a squeak.
runners.comment: Ended last season with his second win of the campaign at Beverley in September.
runners.owner: Gordon Bulloch & S Hardcastle D Szepler

runners.name: Jam Lass
runners.spotlight: Sprang a surprise when winning over C&D (good; 25-1) last October and didn't go on from that in two runs on AW the following month; cannot discount back at this track.
runners.comment: Sprang a surprise when winning over C&D last October.

runners.name: Character Testing
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden hurdler who returned to the Flat following 20-month absence when seventh of nine at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) 13 days ago; needs to come down the weights.
runners.comment: Returned to the Flat following 20-month absence when seventh at Pontefract 13 days ago.

runners.name: Imperial Trooper
runners.spotlight: Sole success came when landing a Ffos Las maiden over 7.5f on heavy for Ed Walker in August 2024; has run well on his last three starts on AW (beaten a neck last time) and if he can reproduce that form on turf then he holds claims.
runners.comment: Has run well on his last three starts on AW, beaten a neck last time.

runners.name: Dawn Of Liberation
runners.spotlight: Won three times last summer and ran well when placed subsequently; well held when tenth of 12 on his return at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) three weeks ago and is still 2lb higher than his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Won three times last summer but only tenth on return to action three weeks ago.

runners.name: Not Me
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden whose form has dipped in three starts for new yard, latest when fifth of nine on return to action at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) 13 days ago; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden whose form has dipped in three starts for new yard.

runners.name: Urban Road
runners.spotlight: Eight of his nine wins have been on AW; can be slowly away and is 2lb above his last winning mark; needs to come down the weights.
runners.comment: Eight of his nine wins have been on AW; can be slowly away.
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Nicator
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three runs over 1m last term; steps up in trip on handicap debut at a low level and may well outclass this opposition.
runners.comment: Showed promise in three runs over 1m last term.

races.race_name: Watch Race Replays At racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Belling The Cat
runners.spotlight: Showed her first worthwhile form when runner-up at Newcastle (6f, AW) on her handicap debut last month; has something to build on returning to turf and should run well.
runners.comment: First worthwhile form when runner-up at Newcastle on her handicap debut last month.

runners.name: Watchdog
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Newcastle (6f, AW) in December but 9lb hike looks to have dented his progress judged on his two runs since.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at Newcastle in December but 9lb hike looks to have dented his progress.
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Mystical Land
runners.spotlight: 0-9, his best run when second to an unexposed rival over 5f at Newcastle AW in January; beaten 26l on his return to turf at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) 13 days ago and has slipped to a career-low mark; doesn't look ready to capitalize.
runners.comment: 0-9, his best run when second to an unexposed rival over 5f at Newcastle AW in January.

runners.name: Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his nursery debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) last September; met issues leaving the stalls in a couple of runs back there since and if he can break better then he comes into the reckoning back on turf.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on his nursery debut at Newcastle last September.

runners.name: Miss Boyd
runners.spotlight: Some promise on turf last August; struggled in handicaps since and tongue-tie worn when last of 14 at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) is left off; not one to give up on.
runners.comment: Some promise on turf last August but has struggled in handicaps since.

runners.name: Gunalt Wavelength
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but runner-up on three occasions last year; handicapper has given her a chance and interesting if the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden but runner-up on three occasions last year.

runners.name: Long Shot
runners.spotlight: Modest form in four starts, failing to improve for the switch to a nursery at Newcastle (6f, AW) when last seen in October; first-time cheekpieces need to have positive effect.
runners.comment: Modest form in four starts last year; first-time cheekpieces need to have positive effect.

runners.name: Muvable
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three runs last term; sports first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut; best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Showed little in three runs last term; sports first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut.

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Alazwar
runners.spotlight: Landed 1m2f amateurs' handicap at Newbury last summer and in better form than his recent form figures suggests, not best placed when 15th in 1m Doncaster handicap last time; needs considering off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Not best placed when 15th at Doncaster last time; needs considering off a slipping mark

runners.name: Chico Dulce
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways over C&D in November and resumed from four months off with a solid fourth of nine in 1m Kempton handicap 19 days ago; he can go very well with that run under his belt with Simon Walker up.
runners.comment: Off four months before solid Kempton fourth latest; can go very well with Simon Walker up

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Scored at Southwell (7f) in January and he's continued in good form, doing best of the pacesetters when third of 14 in 1m Yarmouth handicap nine days ago; he ought to be in the shake-up again in refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Scored at Southwell in January and continued in good form; ought to be in the shake-up

runners.name: Thursday
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m2f/11.5f winner in 2024 but she failed to fire in a pair of handicaps here last winter; back from 13 months off with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: A dual winner in 2024; back from 13 months off with her fitness to prove

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who comes here on the back of a good second of nine in 7f handicap here 26 days ago; must enter calculations off the same mark.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; good second in 7f handicap here latest; must enter calculations off same mark

runners.name: Port Louis
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m winner for Noel Meade in 2025 who caught the eye for his new yard when a running-on second of 22 in 1m amateurs' handicap at Doncaster 22 days ago; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Caught eye for new yard when second at Doncaster latest; very much one to consider

runners.name: Rosemary's Rose
runners.spotlight: In excellent form for her new yard on the AW this winter, scoring over C&D and at Kempton (1m); only ninth back on turf in 1m Nottingham handicap last time but no surprise to see her bounce back here.
runners.comment: In excellent form on AW this winter; only ninth at Nottingham latest but can bounce back

runners.name: Tam Lin
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark for his current yard at Chelmsford (7f) in November and has continued in decent nick, third of seven in 7f Southwell handicap 40 days ago; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Comes here in decent nick, third in 7f Southwell handicap 40 days ago; no forlorn hope

runners.name: Little She
runners.spotlight: Yet to register a victory but she ended 2025 in good nick, second of 13 in 9.5f Wolverhampton in October; she has gone well off a break too so needs considering.
runners.comment: Yet to register a victory but she ended 2025 in good nick; has gone well off a break too

runners.name: King Of Speed
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with victory at Wolverhampton (8.5f) in December and largely in good nick this term, a close fifth in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap two weeks ago; not ruled out off a 2lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Largely in good nick this term; not ruled out off a 2lb lower mark here

runners.name: Showmedemoney
runners.spotlight: In excellent form for Iain Jardine this winter, signing off with 1m Newcastle win in February; more is needed for his new yard off a 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Ended time for Iain Jardine with Newcastle win; more needed for new yard off 4lb higher

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Dyrholaey
runners.spotlight: Can lead; five AW wins at 6f and 5f; has settled into a handicapping groove since the latest of them in February and although he wasn't far away under Harry Vigors at Wolverhampton (6f; blinkers replaced cheekpieces) 11 days ago he isn't obviously ahead of his mark.
runners.comment: Five AW wins elsewhere over 6f/5f; fair run latest but not obviously ahead of his mark

runners.name: Dr Strangelove
runners.spotlight: Below best on soft in October and gelded since; had looked suited by the stiff 6f at Salisbury when winning there previously and although he won over this trip on his Kempton AW debut last spring, today's sharp test may be on the inadequate side; still has some potential, though.
runners.comment: Below best on soft in October but good 6f turf winner previously and has won over 6f on AW

runners.name: Twilight Jet
runners.spotlight: Smart in his prime; not the same force now but the pick of his 6f form on turf and AW in 2025 suggests he's well treated if the race happens to fall his way and this is an easier grade than he often tackles.
runners.comment: Rather mixed in 2025 but pick of 6f form on turf/AW gives him good claims at the weights

runners.name: Hoodie Hoo
runners.spotlight: Has gone well fresh, having run well in sole 2yo start and a wide-margin winner on Wolverhampton AW (6f; made all) on reappearance last year; put up a bold show against the older horses in a competitive 6f race at Nottingham in October and looks a likely type for this race.
runners.comment: Wide-margin AW winner over 6f on reappearance as 3yo; useful turf form in the autumn
runners.owner: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff

runners.name: Rare Change
runners.spotlight: Ran well at Kempton in sole 6f AW run (as 2yo) but proved a far better 3yo, winning three of his first four turf starts, over 6f and 7f; his form deserted him later but he returns to action only just above his latest winning mark last June; interesting.
runners.comment: Ran well in sole 6f AW run; won 3 of first 4 turf runs in 2025; near latest winning mark
runners.owner: Mrs J Woods And K Lawrence

races.race_name: Joan Butcher 90th Birthday Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Phaedra
runners.spotlight: Two of three turf wins at about this trip in 2025 came off today's mark and higher; pleasing start for new yard over 1m at Southwell last month and she was slightly checked in her run when only just losing out there (1m3f) 18 days ago; interesting weights chance.
runners.comment: Won off this mark and higher on turf (1m4f) in 2025; only just failed on Tapeta latest

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Tough mare who added three more wins at about 1m4f last year and made a successful return to the AW when clinging on in an amateur jockeys' race over C&D in February; faded into third after making the running at Wolverhampton since and needs best form to win today.
runners.comment: Three 1m4f wins last year; just hung on for C&D success in January; needs best form to win

runners.name: Schemaya
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to hang on to her lead when making handicap debut at Windsor (extended 1m3f) last May; has run some sound races since, including on Tapeta (1m6f) in October, and perhaps just needed the run at Kempton (1m3f) 12 days ago; shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Winning handicap debut on turf (about 1m3f); some sound efforts since; not far away

runners.name: Rose Cotton
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to a handicap when seizing the initiative early for a wide-margin AW win at Wolverhampton (about 1m1f) in January; couldn't pull off a 6lb rise against a course specialist there last month but she still gave it plenty and this new trip is plausible on pedigree.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m1f); solid run since; new trip
runners.owner: Hasmonean Racing, C Fahy And Marchants

runners.name: Scarlet Sunset
runners.spotlight: Two Wolverhampton wins (about 1m and 1m1f) last midwinter; no sign of her flagging when a strong-finishing third over 1m4f there in October, in only her second attempt at the trip; however, that was her sole run in the last 12 months so there are some readiness queries; cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Two Wolverhampton wins at 1m and 1m1f; stays 1m4f there; run only once in last 12 months

runners.name: Twilight Moon
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over inadequate 1m on comeback last year but she came into her own when upped to 1m4f in the summer, winning back-to-back turf races; had seemed rather becalmed after but she won nicely over C&D when last seen in December; should be further openings for her off 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Won back-to-back 1m4f races on turf last summer; ready success over C&D in December

races.race_name: Follow @AtTheRaces On Instagram Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Golden Brown
runners.spotlight: Son of Galileo Gold who was much improved on his final run in 2025 when second of 15 in Two-Year-Old Trophy at Redcar in October; holds leading form claims on his Polytrack debut in his bid to get off the mark at the eighth attempt.
runners.comment: Much improved when second in Two-Year-Old Trophy at Redcar in October; leading form claims

runners.name: Rainbow Sorbet
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging start when third in 1m Gowran maiden last May for Michael O'Callaghan but beat only one for her new handler after ten months off in C&D novice 26 days ago; this Kodiac filly has something to prove now.
runners.comment: Beat one for new yard in C&D novice 26 days ago; this Kodiac filly has something to prove

runners.name: Zoulette
runners.spotlight: Built on debut sixth when third of five in a first-time hood (retained) in 5f Wolverhampton maiden 20 days ago; this daughter of Zoustar can do better still, especially once qualified for handicaps.
runners.comment: Built on debut when third at Wolverhampton latest; can do better still in the longer term

runners.name: Behike
runners.spotlight: 2-1, went very freely but still made a promising start when third of seven in maiden at Ascot (7f, soft) on his debut in September; this son of Night Of Thunder fetched 500,000gns as a yearling and seems sure to do better, especially if proving more tractable.
runners.comment: Promising debut Ascot third in September; cost 500,000gns as yearling; sure to do better

runners.name: Accommodation
runners.spotlight: Palace Pier filly who cut little ice in 7f Chelmsford maiden (for William Haggas) in November and 6f Kempton novice two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Cut little ice at Chelmsford (for William Haggas) in November and at Kempton latest

runners.name: Kyoto
runners.spotlight: Debut C&D second (for Andrew Balding) in October; gelded and took a step forward for his new yard when third of five in another C&D novice in December; he may do better still.
runners.comment: Gelded and took a step forward when third over C&D in December; may do better still

runners.name: Tass
runners.spotlight: 80-1, beat only one on his debut in 6f Kempton novice six months ago; needs to take a major step forward on his return.
runners.comment: Beat one on his debut in 6f Kempton novice six months ago; lots more required

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Archer's Grace
runners.spotlight: Easy to back for debut at Kempton (7f; 12-1) in October but she came home with a wet sail to take second of nine; had a query about the drop to 6f there on her recent comeback and she wasn't seen to best advantage but likely to progress with racing and the return to 7f can suit.
runners.comment: Promising 7f debut as 2yo; not seen to best advantage over 6f in March; can progress at 7f
runners.owner: Fred Archer Racing - Wheel Of Fortune II

runners.name: Polka Blue
runners.spotlight: Three fair efforts over 6f and 7f on AW and turf as 2yo; saw daylight early and it went to her head on reappearance at Southwell (7f), proving hard to settle, so did well in the circumstances; should fare better.
runners.comment: Promising 6f and 7f runs as 2yo; useful 7f comeback considering she raced freely early

runners.name: Startled Lady
runners.spotlight: Came closest to a win when a neck runner-up on Southwell AW (6f) last July but she didn't have the clearest of runs when finishing fourth of 14 in a C&D handicap (first-time hood) in October; needs a bit more but that's on the cards.
runners.comment: Acquitted herself well at 6f and over C&D as 2yo; has possibilities

runners.name: Nefertari
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; by Oasis Dream; winning siblings include 6f-7.5f winner Luxor (including AW; RPR 91) and 5f AW/6f turf winner Bibendum (74); dam 6f 2yo winner (71), half-sister to 5f/6f Group 1 winner Reverence; interesting pedigree but the fitting of a hood raises early questions.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree, including for AW, but the fitting of a hood raises early questions

runners.name: Moravian
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros foal; second foal; by Supremacy; dam unplaced 5f-1m (RPR 54) but had some useful siblings, including 5f Listed winner Riskit Fora Biskit (dam of smart 6f winner Asjad) and winners at 6f and 7f; worth a market check but lack of experience could cost her.
runners.comment: Encouraging pedigree and worth a market check but lack of experience could cost her

runners.name: Wadirumm
runners.spotlight: Never out of rear division on Wolverhampton debut in March; contrasting tactics when attempting to make all at Southwell (both 7f) later that month but she faded; has work to do at this level.
runners.comment: Both runs at 7f on Tapeta, fading after attempting to make all last month

runners.name: Thimble
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in two 7f runs this year; open to improvement but pedigree gives a firm indication that longer trips will be ideal; worth keeping an eye on with a view to handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Signs of ability at 7f; bred to be suited by 1m2f+; eligible for handicaps soon

runners.name: Curtain Caller
runners.spotlight: 5-1 for debut on Southwell AW (7f) in March where she went the gallop for a fair way but dropped right out in the closing stages; may last longer today but early indications are that she's bound for handicaps.
runners.comment: Showed early pace on 7f debut at Southwell but dropped right out in closing stages

runners.name: Crimson Sunset
runners.spotlight: Sprint pedigree; expectations were non-existent for Salisbury debut (7f; 80-1) in October but she did show a glimmer of ability in a remote fourth; can do better as a 3yo but perhaps not today.
runners.comment: Sprint pedigree; 80-1, glimmer of ability over 7f on Salisbury debut as 2yo; other days

races.race_name: Follow @AtTheRaces On X Handicap

runners.name: Magna
runners.spotlight: Career-high mark following her third 6f win since December, at Wolverhampton in March (made all; Rossa Ryan up for the first time), but she has early speed and looks worth another go at 5f; should make a good fist of it.
runners.comment: Career-high mark following latest 6f win (third since December); worth another go at 5f

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won three times at Wolverhampton last year, at 7f, 6f and 5f; in the frame in three of five starts there since November and this looks a bit easier than his latest fourth there last time; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Front-runs; three Wolverhampton wins in 2025, including at 5f; fair effort there latest
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Stapleford Park
runners.spotlight: Sole win at 7f on turf as 2yo but showed bright early speed back at 5f when losing out late over C&D in March; others might press for the lead here but he's on the same reduced mark; can go well from rail draw.
runners.comment: Sole win over 7f but showed bright speed before headed late over C&D latest; same mark

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last April but that remains her sole win and she's mostly struggled at both sprint trips this year; looks up against it.
runners.comment: C&D winner last April; mostly well held this year and looks up against it

runners.name: Lion Ring
runners.spotlight: Two sprint wins from last four classified starts on Tapeta; multiple Polytrack wins, too, including over C&D, but below best at Wolverhampton last month and regular rider Ryan Kavanagh defects to Stapleford Park.
runners.comment: Two classified wins this year; regular rider Ryan Kavanagh partners Stapleford Park

runners.name: Charging Bull
runners.spotlight: Failed to go on for Andrew Balding but he appreciated the return to 5f at Wolverhampton (cheekpieces replaced hood; tongue-tie added) on third run for this yard, leading before halfway but reeled in late; hanging on to his lead may be difficult again.
runners.comment: Appreciated the return to 5f when runner-up latest; race may not be run to suit

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: Latest AW win was back in 2021 and he dabbles only intermittently on sand these days (finished a long way back at Wolverhampton last month); not beaten far at Bath recently but still has something to prove on first Polytrack run for 12 months.
runners.comment: Latest AW win in 2021; fair run at Bath latest; first Polytrack run for 12 months

runners.name: Balon d'Or
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins at 5f and 6f, the latest off this mark in September; big prices and well held in four sprints here for new yard and, although this is a drop in grade, he may be best left until back on grass.
runners.comment: Three turf wins at 5f/6f, the latest off this mark; well held on AW for new yard

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: The Hare Rail
runners.spotlight: Is 0-15 but arrives in good form, fourth of 11 in 1m Bath handicap 15 days ago; not taken lightly at these weights on his first go over 1m2f.
runners.comment: Is 0-15 but arrives in good form; not taken lightly on his first go over 1m2f
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

runners.name: King's Hand
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Sir Mark Prescott with C&D win in January; changed hands for 32,000gns after and running well for his new yard, going freely when fourth of eight in 1m3f handicap at Kempton last month; in the mix back at this shorter trip.
runners.comment: In good form for new yard, fourth at Kempton latest; well in the mix back in trip here

runners.name: Double Red
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner (at up to 1m2f) in 2025 but he signed off with a below-par fifth in 1m2f Nottingham handicap in October; has won off a break though.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with a below-par fifth at Nottingham in October; has won off a break though

runners.name: Harlington
runners.spotlight: Thriving for his current yard and he completed a hat-trick in 1m2f Chelmsford handicap in February; up another 4lb but this C&D winner rates a player in his bid for a four-timer.
runners.comment: Thriving, and gained hat-trick at Chelmsford in February; this C&D winner rates a player

runners.name: Golden Circet
runners.spotlight: A dual 1m/1m2f winner in 2025 who arrives in decent nick, fourth over hurdles at Plumpton 42 days ago; has since had breathing surgery and visor goes back on here.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth over hurdles at Plumpton latest; since had wind surgery; visor back on
runners.owner: W Trenchard, A Coupland, Cheam Marketng

runners.name: Victors Spirit
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper performer; hooded and much improved on his handicap debut when scoring at Southwell (1m3f) nine days ago; up 3lb but must enter calculations with the headgear retained.
runners.comment: Much improved on handicap debut when scoring at Southwell latest; must enter calculations
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Forca Timao
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and he's yet to hit top form this season, going keenly when a fading fourth of 11 over C&D in February; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: On a losing run and he's yet to hit top form this season; needs to take a step forward

runners.name: Edward Sexton
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper winner; has been brought along steadily in this sphere, a patiently ridden seventh in 1m4f Kempton novice 26 days ago; looks capable of a fair bit better now going handicapping and not without interest.
runners.comment: Useful bumper winner; has been brought along steadily and interesting on his h'cap debut

runners.name: Spaceport
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (at 1m4f/1m6f) for William Muir and Chris Grassick in summer of 2024; very lightly raced and has shown little since for his current yard though, hooded when last of seven in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap 27 days ago; Polytrack debutant.
runners.comment: Shown little for current yard, last at Wolverhampton 27 days ago; Polytrack debutant

runners.name: Perfect Scoundrel
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has shown fair form to date, posting a respectable fourth of nine on his handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f) in September; he may do better still on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth at Sandown in September; may do better still on his seasonal return
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

runners.name: Lemon Muffin
runners.owner: Aaron Sones, Julie Gilbert & Harrison M Sones

runners.name: Lemon Muffin
